description_long: This object is a detailed model of a shrimp-like crustacean, featuring a segmented body with a pronounced cephalothorax and elongated abdomen. The exoskeleton is a pale pinkish-orange, with subtle variations in hue across the body and appendages. Prominent, dark eyes are set on short stalks at the front, flanked by long, slender antennae extending forward. Multiple pairs of delicate, jointed legs are visible beneath the body, along with two larger claws near the head. The tail fans out at the rear, displaying a slightly translucent texture and fine segmentation. The overall surface appears semi-soft with a matte, slightly rough finish, typical of a natural shrimp exoskeleton.
description: A pinkish-orange shrimp-like crustacean with segmented body, long antennae, stalked eyes, and multiple jointed legs including visible claws.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the crustacean, with antennae, legs, and claws clearly visible, and the body’s segmentation and tail fan apparent.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the symmetry of the body, the arrangement of legs and claws, and the spread of the tail fan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with similar visibility of appendages and body segmentation.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the face, with both eyes on stalks, antennae extending forward, and claws visible beneath the head.
